text:
The human-perceived RGB is image 1 and the Tesla AI photon count reconstruction is image 2. 

This is why Tesla FSD can see so well at night or through extreme glare. https://t.co/ttBMzgpJtd
summary: The human-perceived RGB is image 1 and the Tesla AI photon count reconstruction is image 2. This is why Tesla FSD can see so well at night or through extreme glare.
ai_summary: Elon Musk explains Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) superior night vision and glare performance by contrasting human RGB perception with Tesla's AI photon count reconstruction technology. This reveals a key technical advantage in autonomous driving: Tesla's neural network processes raw sensor data differently than human vision, enabling safer operation in adverse lighting conditions—a critical engineering differentiator in the autonomous vehicle market.
embed_html:
The human-perceived RGB is image 1 and the Tesla AI photon count reconstruction is image 2.

This is why Tesla FSD can see so well at night or through extreme glare. pic.twitter.com/ttBMzgpJtd

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 9, 2026

text: Tesla AI Vision deploys airbags before impact, which greatly reduces risk of injury or death. This comes for free on all new cars. https://t.co/828FOgD2SI
summary: Tesla AI Vision deploys airbags before impact, which greatly reduces risk of injury or death. This comes for free on all new cars.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Tesla's AI Vision system now deploys airbags before impact occurs, leveraging predictive AI to prevent injuries before collisions happen. This demonstrates Tesla's competitive advantage in autonomous safety technology and represents a tangible product differentiation that comes standard on all new vehicles—illustrating how AI/ML integration creates measurable business value and reduces liability while enhancing customer safety.
embed_html:
Tesla AI Vision deploys airbags before impact, which greatly reduces risk of injury or death. This comes for free on all new cars. https://t.co/828FOgD2SI

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 9, 2026

text:
Congrats to the @Starlink engineering & production teams on excellent work!

It was great to see everyone when I walked the production line in Redmond on Wednesday 🖤✨
summary: Congrats to the @Starlink engineering & production teams on excellent work! It was great to see everyone when I walked the production line in Redmond on Wednesday 🖤✨
ai_summary: Elon Musk congratulates the Starlink engineering and production teams for their work and shares a personal observation from visiting the production line in Redmond on Wednesday. The post demonstrates leadership visibility and team recognition practices, highlighting Starlink's ongoing manufacturing operations and the importance of executive engagement with production teams to maintain quality and morale during scaling phases.
embed_html:
Congrats to the @Starlink engineering & production teams on excellent work!

text: It was an honor to be shown the awesome @Intel fab in Oregon this week. Looking forward to a great partnership with @SpaceX & @Tesla!
summary: It was an honor to be shown the awesome @Intel fab in Oregon this week. Looking forward to a great partnership with @SpaceX & @Tesla!
ai_summary: Elon Musk visited Intel's fabrication facility in Oregon and announced a forthcoming partnership involving SpaceX and Tesla. This signals potential collaboration on semiconductor manufacturing and advanced chip production, likely relevant to Tesla's autonomous driving compute needs and SpaceX's avionics/control systems, demonstrating how vertically integrated hardware companies are securing domestic semiconductor supply chains and manufacturing partnerships.
embed_html:
It was an honor to be shown the awesome @Intel fab in Oregon this week. Looking forward to a great partnership with @SpaceX & @Tesla!
